__doc__: REL-6 composite-index ADDITIONS for Arbor Firestore.
THIS FILE IS NOT THE DEPLOYABLE FILE. It is a merge artifact.
The live, deployable file is /firestore.indexes.json at the repo root.
Merge the two objects under `__additions__.indexes` into the `indexes`
array of the live file (append; do not reorder; do not remove the existing
memoryEvents (childId ASC, createdAt ASC) COLLECTION_GROUP index — it backs
the live listEvents() query in firestoreMemoryStore.ts:23 and removing it
reproduces the prior Gemini-outage COLLECTION_GROUP index bug).
After merge, the root file's `indexes` array must contain THREE entries:
1. memoryEvents (childId ASC, createdAt ASC)  <- EXISTING, keep verbatim
2. memoryEvents (childId ASC, createdAt DESC) <- ADD (pre-emptive: desc UI sort)
3. consultRequests (ownerUid ASC, createdAt DESC) <- ADD (pre-emptive: desc pagination)
Both additions are PRE-EMPTIVE. As of 2026-06-17 the only composite-requiring
query in the codebase (memoryEvents childId+createdAt ASC) is already covered.
consultRequests.ts:58 (where ownerUid ==) and the listEvents() asc query sort
client-side / use the existing index, so neither addition is required to ship
today. They are declared NOW so that when the near-term UI features land
(reverse-chronological consult inbox; reverse-chronological memory timeline)
the query does not fail at runtime with a FAILED_PRECONDITION 'needs index'
error on grown data. Indexes are additive and a never-used index is safe to
delete, so pre-declaring carries no correctness risk — only marginal write
amplification + storage.
TTL POLICY (aiQuota.expireAt) — NOT expressible in this file. See __ttl_note__.
Firestore TTL is a field operation set via gcloud, not an index/fieldOverride
doc. firebase deploy --only firestore:indexes will NOT set it. It must be run
by a human with GCP access (see INFRA-SNIPPETS.md applyStep).

__ttl_note__.status_today: field is WRITTEN on every quota window doc, but NO TTL policy exists, so stale per-key+window docs accumulate forever
__ttl_note__.enable_command: gcloud firestore fields ttls update expireAt --collection-group=aiQuota --enable-ttl --project <PROD_PROJECT_ID>
__ttl_note__.verify_command: gcloud firestore fields ttls describe expireAt --collection-group=aiQuota --project <PROD_PROJECT_ID>
__ttl_note__.disable_command_rollback: gcloud firestore fields ttls update expireAt --collection-group=aiQuota --disable-ttl --project <PROD_PROJECT_ID>
__ttl_note__.why: Data-minimisation / storage-limitation control (GDPR/AVG Art.5(1)(e); IL Amendment 13 retention). Auto-purges per-user usage counters once their 24h window closes; net storage REDUCTION. Record in DPIA security-measures.

__memory_retention_note__.decision: NO Firestore TTL is applied to memoryEvents in Wave-0.
rationale: memoryEvents is the product's core durable record (the 'memory moat'); it is NOT ephemeral counter data like aiQuota and must NOT be auto-expired by a blanket TTL.

Retention/erasure for memoryEvents is already handled at the application layer by the GDPR/AVG erase path: routes/api.ts:1559 -> memoryStore.eraseChild(childId) deletes the child's memoryEvents on a Right-to-Erasure request (lib/api.ts:200 exposes /api/privacy/erase).
__memory_retention_note__.future_work: If a fixed-horizon retention policy for memoryEvents is contractually required (e.g. a B2G DPA caps retention at N years post-inactivity), implement it as an application-level scheduled purge keyed on createdAt + last-activity, NOT a raw Firestore TTL on expireAt — because memoryEvents docs intentionally have no expireAt field. Document the chosen horizon in the DPIA. This is out of scope for REL-6 / Wave-0.

__merged_preview__.comment: This is what /firestore.indexes.json should look like AFTER the human merge. Copy this whole object (minus this comment and the __ wrapper keys above) over the root file, OR hand-append the two __additions__ objects. fieldOverrides stays [].
